title: VindPod
status: Archive material ingested from supplied PDF
summary: VindPod is presented here as a JungDesign development project: an example of David Jung’s long-practised ability to move from idea and problem-framing into a designed proposition that can be explained, reviewed and refined.
note: The supplied PDF is a scanned six-page document, so the original pages are preserved as project plates while the editable web copy can be refined as more information arrives.

title: Project Management
status: Archive material ingested from supplied DOCX and PDF
summary: David’s project-management work sits at the practical centre of JungDesign: meeting a client’s expectations, protecting budget and programme, and adding design-management judgement so complex work stays coherent.
note: Source text was extracted from the supplied ProjectManagement1.docx. The companion Projects1.pdf is scanned and has been preserved as project plates.

caseStudies.title: AMEX America’s Cup Village, 1998–2000
caseStudies.text: Contracted to design and build hospitality venues across five village locations, including a floating AMEX Yacht Club, corporate chalets, boat-party moorings, public canopy and bar, and entertainment stage. The capped $5m programme was delivered with the final venue opened a week early.

caseStudies.title: Air New Zealand terminal developments
caseStudies.text: Managed North Island regional front-of-house terminal rebuilds and Air New Zealand terminal/sales-office reimaging work across Australia, Japan, Singapore and Taiwan, including new-destination delivery in Taiwan under a three-month design/build programme.

caseStudies.title: Air New Zealand self-service kiosks
caseStudies.text: Surveyed 25 New Zealand airports to support end-to-end self-service ticketing, then later managed replacement of ageing kiosks with new French-built units across an 18-month, approximately $4m programme.

caseStudies.title: BNZ / ANZ bank reimaging
caseStudies.text: Rolled out BNZ interiors across the East Coast of the North Island, then extended the work into ANZ/National Bank relocation and reimaging programmes across the same region.

caseStudies.title: Telstra Australia
caseStudies.text: Reviewed and value-managed Telstra retail interior concepts, reducing budget pressure before leading a team of project managers through the rollout of around 200 live-phone retail stores across Australia.

title: Product Development
status: Archive material ingested from supplied DOCX and PDF
summary: JungDesign’s product-development history shows David working from practical problems toward commercially produced objects and systems: measuring, dispensing, protecting, installing, reducing risk and making use easier.
note: Source text was extracted from ProductDevelopment1.docx. The companion Products2.pdf is scanned and has been preserved as project plates.

caseStudies.title: 5 litre measuring jug
caseStudies.text: Developed for Peak Products in 1986 with attention to stackability for export, raised calibration for readability and dual-spout pourability for dry and wet contents. The product has remained in production for an international market.

caseStudies.title: EYEQue airport security system
caseStudies.text: A proposed AI-enabled front-of-house airport security system developed with Fingermark Global after David identified a security-system fault during Air New Zealand terminal work. The system was developed, trialled and tested over approximately 18 months.

caseStudies.title: Hospital drug-bag labelling system
caseStudies.text: A quick, accurate labelling concept for DHB anaesthetists, developed to reduce waste and improve legibility when opened drug bags lost their original identification during surgical routines.

caseStudies.title: The Egg Splitter System (TESS)
caseStudies.text: Created with McDonald’s New Zealand and Australia to reduce salmonella risk by allowing eggs to move through restaurant handling and processing without staff touching the shell.

caseStudies.title: Ericsson phone system casing
caseStudies.text: Designed a casing for Ericsson’s phone splitter system for dense lower-economic South Pacific communities, enabling main-line connection and multiple sub-lines from standard wooden power poles.

caseStudies.title: VPS — Vine Pod System
caseStudies.text: A work-in-progress JungDesign concept for safe, precise herbicide delivery to pest vines, developed with interest from WWF New Zealand, Auckland Council biodiversity teams and Unitec.
